scope: Public statements and project-page text only. Use for evidence grounding, not impersonation.

quotes.quote: I reverse engineered San Francisco's parking ticket system to show you exactly where each parking cop last wrote a ticket.
quotes.interpretation: Directly states the technical method and one-screen utility. Use as evidence for the pattern of turning a public system into a legible map.

quotes.quote: This is culture surveillance. No one notices, no one consents. But it's not about catching criminals. It's about catching vibes.
quotes.interpretation: Shows self-aware, provocative framing around public sensing and consent. Use with safety caveats.

quotes.quote: The model is certainly biased. It's certainly flawed. But we judge places by the people who go there... A mirror held up to our collective vanity.
quotes.interpretation: Evidence of explicitly acknowledging flaws and bias while framing the work as social commentary. This project is a high-risk anti-pattern for replication.

quotes.quote: You have to follow through on your ideas because if you don't, you might stop having them. It's like a muscle you have to keep using.
quotes.interpretation: Core process quote: execution is treated as part of idea generation, not merely delivery.

quotes.quote: I start them over the course of the weekend and just put them out there and see what happens.
quotes.interpretation: Defines the weekend-scale prototyping pattern and launch-as-experiment behavior.
Supports the preference for simple functional labels over grander artistic framing.

quotes.quote: I'm just a normal guy who knows something about technology and likes seeing amusing things.
quotes.interpretation: Evidence for curiosity and amusement as stated public motivation. Do not overextend this into private psychology.
Condenses the emotional value of accidental digital artifacts in the nostalgia projects.

quotes.quote: I figured out that the ticket numbers are predictable, which means I can efficiently scrape them.
quotes.interpretation: Evidence for system-pattern detection: find the predictable residue, then build the artifact.

quotes.quote: I'm not trying to make a grand political statement. I think this is really cool data to look at.
quotes.interpretation: Supports the stated public framing of data curiosity rather than explicit political activism.

quotes.quote: i found millions of YouTube videos that have default camera names as titles (like IMG_0276) and made it into a website where you can watch random ones. unedited, pure moments from random lives
quotes.interpretation: Canonical example of the found-data-to-website launch structure.

quotes.quote: I'm Riley, a distance runner from New York. I made Routeshuffle to help with my summer training in high school.
quotes.interpretation: Shows that personal needs can seed small public tools.

quotes.quote: I just thought it was kind of a funny little joke to pick up songs instead of gunshots.
quotes.interpretation: Evidence for humor as idea generation and surveillance inversion.

quotes.quote: This site only worked for 4 hours until the SFMTA cut the data source I was using. This data is a snapshot of how it looked on Sept. 25, 2025 at noon.
quotes.interpretation: Evidence that ephemerality and snapshot preservation are part of the public project lifecycle.

quotes.quote: we cloned Gmail, except you're logged in as Epstein and can see his emails
quotes.interpretation: Shows the familiar-interface reframe pattern. Must be adapted carefully for sensitive public records.
